RootCauses.Evidence: Repository methods load all matching FabricRoll rows into memory, then aggregate/project in C#. Current 200K test confirms full-result operations dominate versus LIMIT 50.
RootCauses.Solution: Server-side pagination and DTO projection, composite indexes on WarehouseId/Status/RemainingLengthMeters/RollNumber, and WPF DataGrid virtualization.

RootCauses.Evidence: GetFabricStockBalancesAsync loads warehouse_stocks and all rolls, then repeatedly filters rolls per stock row in memory.
RootCauses.Solution: Move aggregation to SQL GROUP BY or materialized inventory summary, fetch only top/page rows for the current tab.

RootCauses.Evidence: The mobile page issues warehouses/dashboard/alerts calls. Backend aggregation is the main cost now; on 3G payload and latency add visible delay.
RootCauses.Solution: Keep calls parallel, add response compression, cache dashboard snapshots, and paginate any future roll list endpoint.

Questions.desktop_rows_displayed: Current repository shape returns all rows for the selected warehouse; no server page contract exists for GetFabricRollsAsync.
Questions.wpf_virtualization: Must be verified visually in XAML/runtime; code-level performance risk remains because ItemsSource can receive tens of thousands of rows.
Questions.filter_location: Inventory filtering in tested repository methods is server-side only for simple WHERE clauses, but aggregation and many projections run client-side in C#.
Questions.ef_loading: No lazy-loading proxy was found; slowness is from explicit ToListAsync full loads and follow-up dictionary/lookups.
Questions.sql_executed: EXPLAIN ANALYZE output is included in the JSON report.
Questions.web_api_calls_inventory_page: 3 calls in web-client Inventory.tsx: warehouses, dashboard, alerts.
Questions.web_parallel_or_sequential: React Query calls are declared independently and can run in parallel.
Questions.web_pagination: Current inventory web page does not request fabric-roll pages; WPF/repository roll APIs are unpaged.
Questions.compression: API Program.cs does not configure response compression.
Questions.json_response_size: Measured in payloadBytes per web measurement in the JSON report.
Questions.fabric_roll_indexes: Existing model defines indexes only on Barcode and FabricBatchId; no WarehouseId/Status composite index.
Questions.partial_index_complete: No partial index for available/complete rolls exists in the current model.
Questions.columns_returned: Several paths return full entities first, then map to DTOs.
Questions.includes: No large Include chain was found in the inventory repository; related data is loaded with separate lookup queries.
Questions.n_plus_one: Warehouse list uses per-warehouse stock/roll queries; operations center uses in-memory repeated filtering across loaded collections.
Questions.lookup_cache: Catalog lookups are queried per operation; no cross-request cache is configured.
